skill.name: unreal-engine-cpp-pro
skill.description: Guidance for Unreal Engine 5 C++ development, including UObject safety, reflection macros, performance patterns, and Blueprint integration.
skill.summary: Write safer and faster Unreal Engine 5 C++ with practical project patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code spans, Unreal Engine C++ identifiers, and ordinary example code. No prompt injection, malware intent, credential access, network exfiltration, or executable helper script was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown code spans flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections in SKILL.md are Markdown inline code and C++ fenced examples. They document Unreal Engine APIs and naming conventions; they do not execute shell commands or instruct the agent to run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are documentation code spans and C++ examples inside SKILL.md. No script file, command runner, shell invocation, or user-input command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to descriptive text and a C++ comment. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain prose or comments, not cryptographic code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: False positive: System reconnaissance in Unreal lifecycle examples
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections in ExampleActor.cpp and ExampleActor.h are Unreal Engine lifecycle methods, comments, logging, and player-controller lookup. They inspect game runtime objects, not host system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantics are ordinary Unreal Engine Actor callbacks and gameplay references. No OS, environment, filesystem, network, or hardware enumeration is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: System reconnaissance in Unreal guidance text
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections in SKILL.md are Unreal Engine coding guidance and example snippets. The lines discuss casting, component lookup, asset references, loading examples, and delegate cleanup, not host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is domain-specific Unreal Engine advice. No evidence found of system profiling, host fingerprinting, or data collection intent.

content.value_statement: Unreal Engine C++ projects often fail through garbage collection mistakes, hard asset references, or excessive Tick usage. This skill gives focused guidance for safer UObject code, Blueprint exposure, and performance-aware patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains Unreal Engine 5 C++ class, struct, enum, interface, and boolean naming conventions.
Guides safe UObject member handling with UPROPERTY, IsValid checks, and garbage collection awareness.
Recommends performance patterns such as disabling Tick by default and caching references outside hot paths.
Shows how to expose C++ classes, properties, functions, and interfaces to Unreal reflection and Blueprints.
Provides example Actor code using components, soft class references, transient cached pointers, and lifecycle methods.
Covers debugging practices with UE_LOG, on-screen messages, and Visual Logger references.
limitations: It targets Unreal Engine 5.x and may not match older engine versions.
It does not replace profiling, engine documentation, or project-specific architecture review.
It is not designed for Blueprint-only projects without C++ code.
It provides guidance and examples, not a complete gameplay framework.

use_cases.title: Review Actor Lifecycle Code
use_cases.description: Check constructors, BeginPlay, Tick, and EndPlay usage for Unreal Engine conventions and avoidable runtime costs.

use_cases.title: Prepare Blueprint APIs
use_cases.description: Design C++ properties and functions for controlled Blueprint access while keeping gameplay state protected.

use_cases.title: Reduce Asset Load Problems
use_cases.description: Identify hard references and replace them with soft reference patterns where they are appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Simple Actor
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a new C++ Actor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Unreal Engine 5 C++ Actor for naming, UPROPERTY usage, Tick settings, and Blueprint exposure. Explain the most important fixes first.

prompt_templates.title: Improve UObject Safety
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of object references and garbage collection.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Unreal Engine C++ class for UObject lifetime issues. Focus on UPROPERTY coverage, nullable references, IsValid usage, and transient cached pointers.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Runtime Patterns
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced performance pass for gameplay code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Unreal Engine 5 gameplay code for Tick work, repeated casting, component lookup, hard references, and synchronous loading risks. Suggest practical alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production API
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced design of C++ systems exposed to Blueprints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help design an Unreal Engine C++ API for this gameplay system. Include reflection macros, Blueprint access levels, soft references, lifecycle hooks, and debugging points.

output_examples.input: Review a character class that stores raw UObject pointers and performs component lookup every frame.
output_examples.output: The review highlights garbage collection exposure, repeated lookup costs, and Tick overuse. It recommends tracked properties, cached references, and event-driven updates.

output_examples.input: Plan Blueprint access for a weapon configuration system.
output_examples.output: The response separates designer-editable defaults from runtime state, recommends read-only Blueprint access for protected state, and suggests soft references for large assets.

output_examples.input: Explain why a hard class reference causes unexpected asset loads.
output_examples.output: The explanation describes Unreal reference chains, when soft class pointers help, and when synchronous loading should be avoided or replaced with async loading.

best_practices: Provide the target Unreal Engine version and relevant class headers when asking for review.
Ask for tradeoffs when exposing C++ state to Blueprints, especially for runtime gameplay data.
Pair the guidance with profiling results before changing performance-sensitive systems.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to support Blueprint-only workflows without C++ context.
Do not use generated suggestions without compiling against your exact Unreal Engine version.
Do not treat generic performance advice as proof without measurement in your project.

faq.question: Does this skill write complete Unreal Engine plugins?
faq.answer: It can guide C++ structure and patterns, but it is not a complete plugin generator.

faq.question: Which Unreal Engine version does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Unreal Engine 5.x C++ development.

faq.question: Can it help with Blueprint exposure?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers reflection macros and safer Blueprint access choices for C++ systems.

faq.question: Can it diagnose garbage collection bugs?
faq.answer: It can identify common UObject reference and UPROPERTY risks, but runtime diagnosis still needs project testing.

faq.question: Does it run external build commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The skill content provides guidance and examples, not executable scripts.

faq.question: Is it suitable for non-Unreal C++ projects?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is specific to Unreal Engine C++ conventions and APIs.
